incident_report:
# Incident Report: Dungeon Agent Run (Seed 1)

Agent A and Agent B failed to complete the dungeon objective, reaching the turn limit of 50 turns (finishing on turn 51) with zero progress toward the goal. Both agents encountered repeated spurious_block anomalies during movement attempts across 12 separate incidents, beginning as early as turn 5 for both agents and continuing throughout the run. The agents never obtained a key pickup, unlocked any doors, or sent messages to coordinate strategy, suggesting they were unable to establish effective navigation or communication patterns despite having 51 turns to operate. The critical failure point appears to be the consistent collision with environmental anomalies rather than pathfinding errors—the sheer frequency of spurious blocks (one every 4-5 turns on average) prevented sustained forward progress. Without evidence of DM interaction or adaptive behavior after encountering the anomalies, the agents appear to have lacked either a recovery mechanism for blocked movement or sufficient coordination to explore alternate routes.
